heading: Assistance from other government agencies
label: 1465d Assistance from other government agencies
notes: Repeal of SectionSection repealed upon transmittal of determination by President under that democratically elected government in Cuba is in power, see .section 6063(c)(3) of this titlesection 6037(c) of this title

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act which comprises this subchapter, and not as part of the United States Information and Educational Exchange Act of 1948 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments1998—Subsec. (a). substituted “Broadcasting Board of Governors” for “United States Information Agency” and “Board” for “Agency”.Pub. L. 105–277Subsec. (b). , substituted “Board” for “Agency” in two places.Pub. L. 105–277, § 1324(2)

children.label: a
children.text: In order to assist the Broadcasting Board of Governors in carrying out the purposes set forth in , any agency or instrumentality of the United States may sell, loan, lease, or grant property (including interests therein) and may perform administrative and technical support and services at the request of the Board. Support and services shall be provided on a reimbursable basis. Any reimbursement shall be credited to the appropriation from which the property, support, or services was derived.section 1465 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: The Board may carry out the purposes of by means of grants, leases, or contracts (subject to the availability of appropriations), or such other means as the Board determines will be most effective.section 1465a of this title
